message:
test(caut-types): pin leaf-crate boundary post extraction (ft-cui2w)

ft-2z15d (0d5cba9f) carved frankenterm-core::caut's plain-data types
into the new frankenterm-core-caut-types/ leaf crate. The crate
shipped with zero tests, so a future change to slug parsing,
camelCase serde aliases, or thiserror format strings would only
surface via the parent crate's downstream consumers — by then the
type-level cause is buried under runtime failures.

Add a #[cfg(test)] mod tests block (12 tests, runs in 7s) at the
canonical home for the types:

  CautService:
    - as_str round-trip for every variant + Display impl
    - provider_arg pins the strings the caut binary actually
      accepts as `--provider <arg>` (codex / claude / gemini —
      flipping any silently re-routes account selection)
    - from_cli_input over every entry in supported_cli_inputs()
    - rejects unknown slugs and the empty string

  Slug helpers:
    - is_openai_slug / is_anthropic_slug / is_google_slug:
      whitespace-trim + ASCII case-fold tolerance
    - cross-helper isolation (claude doesn't match openai, etc.)

  CautError:
    - Display strings match the thiserror #[error(...)]
      declarations for every variant including the structured-
      field ones (Timeout, NonZeroExit, OutputTooLarge, InvalidJson, Io)
    - serde JSON round-trip preserves payload for every variant

  CautUsage / CautRefresh / CautAccountUsage:
    - account_usage_deserializes_caut_camel_case_aliases —
      pins percentRemaining / limitHours / resetAt / tokensUsed /
      tokensRemaining / tokensLimit alias bridges (drop one and
      account selection regresses to 0% remaining for that field)
    - account_usage_unknown_fields_land_in_extra — verifies the
      #[serde(flatten)] HashMap captures vendor-specific keys
    - usage / refresh round-trip preserves accounts + extra
    - default values are empty

Verified: cargo test -p frankenterm-core-caut-types --lib passes
12/12 in 7.02s on /tmp/ft-swarm-violetmoth-target.

Companion bead pending for the cass-types leaf (ft-8cg6y has the
same gap).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
